*�����������������b"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""

from TDStoreTools import StorageManager
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDJSON = op.TDModules.mod.TDJSON

class VRExt:
	"""
	VRExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp

		# attributes:
		self.a = 0 # attribute
		self.B = 1 # promoted attribute

		# properties
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'MyProperty', value=0, dependable=True,
						   readOnly=False)

		# stored items (persistent across saves and re-initialization):
		storedItems = [
			# Only 'name' is required...
			{'name': 'StoredProperty', 'default': None, 'readOnly': False,
			 						'property': True, 'dependable': True},
		]
		# Uncomment the line below to store StoredProperty. To clear stored
		# 	items, use the Storage section of the Component Editor

		# self.stored = StorageManager(self, ownerComp, storedItems)

	def CheckCompliance(self, par, complianceDat):
		"""
		Checks a parameter modification for compliance.
		"""
		owner = par.owner
		#debug(comp)
		#debug(complianceDat.rows('*'))
		compliance = {}
		op = complianceDat
		header = [i.val for i in op.rows(0)[0]]
		#debug('header', header)
		if header == ['Component', 'Local', 'Published']:
			rows = op.rows()
			rows.pop(0)
			for row in rows:
				current = compliance.get(row[0].val) or []
				compliance[row[0].val] = current + [[row[1].val,
														row[2].val]]

					#compliance = {i[0].val: [i[1].val,i[2].val] for i in op.rows()}

		#debug('compliance',compliance)
		if compliance:
			parMaps = compliance.get(par.name)
			pubComp = getattr(owner.par, par.name).eval()
			#debug('maps', parMaps)
			for parMap in parMaps:
				#debug(owner, pubComp, parMap)
				publisher = getattr(owner.par, par.name).eval()
				if publisher:
					published = getattr(publisher.par, parMap[1]).eval()
					#debug(publisher, published)
					if published:
						publishedPath = owner.relativePath(published)
						#debug(publishedPath)
						setattr(owner.par, parMap[0], publishedPath)
					else:
						setattr(owner.par, parMap[0], '')
				else:
					setattr(owner.par, parMap[0], '')

	def WarningWrongRate(self):
		"""
		Pops dialog for wrong rate
		"""
		op.TDResources.op('popDialog').par.Title = 'TDVR Error: Incorrect Rate'
		op.TDResources.op('popDialog').par.Text = 'For optimal performance in VR please set the timeline rate to 90 FPS.'
		op.TDResources.op('popDialog').par.Buttons = 1
		op.TDResources.op('popDialog').par.Buttonlabel1 = 'Ok'
		op.TDResources.op('popDialog').par.Open.pulse()

*�����������������Open VR HMD Component

Open VR HMD component supports the openVR head mounted display system. This system consists of the openVR sensor data stream, an 3D object hierarchy and rendering system that renders direct to the HMD hardware device. 

The HMD object hiearchy has a built in SetTransform() python function that takes a TouchDesigner matrix python object which will stuff a new transform as an offset from the HMD transformRoot object.

An additional transformRig object is available to constrain to a 3D object, for example to move around a 3D world on a vehicle. See the Transform Rig COMP for more information.

Render Settings Parameters

Active: Activates the HMD Device data operators.

Transform Rig COMP: A parent component can be attached to the root of the HMD object hiearchy.  This root position will essentially allow the VR room area to be transformed to the tranform Rig world space position. By default the "drone" transform system is used to provide a way to move the VR room to a new position using an Xbox controller.

Camera Near Far Plane: The minimum and maximum distance from the eye cameras where objects are visbible. Geometry outside this range is culled and therefore not visible.

Camera Fog: Distance attenuated color for the HMD camera to simulate a fog or haze gradiation that is blended with the rendered 3D geometry based based on the distance from the HMD eye cameras. 

Camera Fog Near: The distance from which the fog blending begins.

Camera Fog Far: The distance at which the fog color is at its maximum value.

Camera Fog Color: The color of the fog.

Camaera Fog Alpha: An attenuation value to set the maximum blended value for the color.

Render Lights: The light objects to use to light the 3D scene.

Render Geos: The 3D geometry objects to render in the 3D scene.

Height Offset: A secondary offset in the HMD object hiearchy in the Y axis. This can be used to finely adjust for offsets from the floor. For example if you are sitting at your desk but want to simulate the eye point of standing or walking.

Reset Transform: Resets the transform of the transformRoot object of the HDM object hieararchy.

Methods

SetTransform(matrix): Extracts the translates from the passed in xform matrix and sets the transform of the root component.

OffsetEyesToRoot(self): The HMD (eyes) are offset from the root transform depending on where you are standing in the safe room. This inverts that offset and puts it into the root pretransform - just working with tx tz for now.

A height offset parameter has been added to this component's custom parameters.  This will add an offset to the Y position after teleportation.

Published OPs:

HMD Root OBJ: The top of the HMD object hiearchy.

HMD World OBJ: The position of the HMD in world space.

HMD Render TOP: Left eye render TOP

*�����������������$"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner component networks with python
functionality. An extension can be accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from
any operator within the extended component. If the extension is "promoted", all
its attributes with capitalized names can be accessed directly through the
extended component, e.g. op('yourComp').ExtensionMethod()
"""

class OpenVRHMDExt:
	"""
	OpenVRHMD description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.rootComp = self.ownerComp.op('transformRoot')
		self.transformRigComp = self.ownerComp.op('transformRig')
		self.eyeComp = self.ownerComp.op('eyeCenter')
		self.sensorChop = self.ownerComp.op('openVRSensor')
		self.tyChan = self.ownerComp.op('openVRSensor')['head:ty']
		self.transformRigParent = '..'
		self.heightOffsetPar = self.ownerComp.par.Heightoffset
		transRigComp = self.ownerComp.par.Transformrigcomp.eval()
		if isinstance(transRigComp, COMP) and \
				isinstance(transRigComp.op('constraint1'), ObjectCOMP):
			transRigComp.op('constraint1')
			self.transformRigParent = transRigComp.op('constraint1')

	def HandleParPulse(self, par):
		"""
		Handles pulse parameter callbacks
		"""
		if par.name == 'Reset':
			self.ResetCameraRig()


	@property
	def TransformRigParent(self):
		"""
		Returns a valid transform rig parent or if not found returns None
		"""
		return self.transformRigParent

	def SetTransform(self, xformM):
		"""
		Extracts the translates from the passed in xform matrix and sets the
		transform of the root component.
		"""
		newS, newR, newT, = xformM.decompose()
		newP = tdu.Vector(newT)
		self.rootComp.setTransform(xformM)
		self.OffsetEyesToRoot()

	def OffsetEyesToRoot(self):
		"""
		The HMD (eyes) are offset from the root transform depending on where you
		are standing in the safe room.  This inverts that offset and puts it
		into the root pretransform - just working with tx tz for now.

		A height offset parameter has been added to this component's custom
		parameters.  This will add an offset to the Y position after
		teleportation.
		"""
		es, er, et = self.eyeComp.worldTransform.decompose()
		eyeP = tdu.Vector(et)

		trigM = self.transformRigComp.worldTransform
		trigs, trigr, trigt = trigM.decompose()
		trigP = tdu.Vector(trigt)


		rs, rr, rt = self.rootComp.worldTransform.decompose()
		rootP = tdu.Vector(rt)

		diffEyeRigP = eyeP-trigP
		hOffset = self.heightOffsetPar.eval()
		height = diffEyeRigP[1] + hOffset
		heightP = tdu.Vector(0,height,0)
		diffRootRig = rootP-trigP
		offsetP = ((trigP - eyeP) + heightP) + diffRootRig
		offsetM = tdu.Matrix()
		offsetM.identity()
		offsetM.translate(offsetP)
		self.rootComp.setPreTransform(offsetM)

	def ResetCameraRig(self):
		offsetM = tdu.Matrix()
		offsetM.identity()
		self.SetTransform(offsetM)

*�����������������
?"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner component networks with python
functionality. An extension can be accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from
any operator within the extended component. If the extension is "promoted", all
its attributes with capitalized names can be accessed directly through the
extended component, e.g. op('yourComp').ExtensionMethod()
"""

import collections

class WorkstationExt:
	"""
	WorkstationExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.monitorsDat = self.ownerComp.op('monitors1') 
		self.valueNames = ['primary', 'left', 'right', 'bottom', 'top', 'width', 'height']
		#self.Monitor = collections.namedtuple('Monitor', self.valueNames)
		self.monitors = []

	def UpdateMonitors(self):
		"""
		Creates a named tuple for each monitor
		"""
		nMonitors = self.monitorsDat.numRows-1
	
		self.monitors = []
		for i in range(nMonitors):
			values = [int(self.monitorsDat[i+1,j].val) for j in self.valueNames]
			monitor = dict(zip(self.valueNames, values))
			self.monitors.append(monitor)

	def SnapToMonitor(self):
		"""
		Snaps the VR monitor to the specified screen index
		"""
		#monIndex = self.ownerComp.par.Capturemonitorindex.eval()
		primaryMon = next(monitor for monitor in self.monitors if monitor['primary'] == 1)
		#captureMon = self.monitors[monIndex]
		minX = min(monitor['left'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		minY = min(monitor['bottom'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		maxX = max(monitor['right'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		maxY = max(monitor['top'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		#debug(minX, minY, maxX, maxY)
		resolution = (maxX-minX, maxY-minY)
		#debug(resolution)

		#Set desktop crop
		left = abs(minX)
		right = left + primaryMon['width']
		bottom = abs(minY)
		top = bottom + primaryMon['height']
		self.SetDesktopCrop(left, right, bottom, top)

		#Set screen crop match default monitor
		left = 0
		right = primaryMon['width']
		bottom = 0
		top = primaryMon['height']
		self.SetScreenCrop(left, right, bottom, top)

	def SetDesktopCrop(self, left, right, bottom, top):
		"""
		Sets the crop settings
		"""
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcropleft = left
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcropright = right
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcropbottom = bottom
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcroptop = top

	def SetScreenCrop(self, left, right, bottom, top):
		"""
		Sets screen crop 
		"""

		self.ownerComp.par.Screencropleft = left
		self.ownerComp.par.Screencropright = right
		self.ownerComp.par.Screencropbottom = bottom
		self.ownerComp.par.Screencroptop = top

functionality. An extension can be accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from
any operator within the extended component. If the extension is "promoted", all

class PickExt:
	"""
	ButtonSwitch description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.CallbackComp = None
		self.InfoPComp = None
		self.SetCallbackComponent()
		self.SetInfoPocessorModule()

	def HandleParPulse(self, par):
		"""
		Handles pulse parameter callbacks
		"""
		print(par.name)
		if par.name == 'Editinfoprocessormodule':
			pMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
			if isinstance(pMod, DAT):
				pMod.par.edit.pulse()

	def SetCallbackComponent(self):
		"""
		Sets the callback component
		"""
		callbackComp = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackcomp.eval()
		if isinstance(callbackComp, COMP): 
			self.CallbackComp = callbackComp
		else:
			self.CallbackComp = None

	def SetInfoPocessorModule(self):
		"""
		Sets the info processor module
		"""
		infoPMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
		if isinstance(infoPMod, DAT): 
			self.InfoPComp = infoPMod
		else:
			self.InfoPComp = None

	def Trigger(self, info):
		"""
		Responds to trigger events from picking system
		"""	
		debug(info)
		callFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackfunctionname.eval()
		procFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessorfunction.eval()
		#debug(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)
		if procFuncName and hasattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName):
			info = getattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)(info)
			if info:
				getattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName)(info)

*�����������������"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner component networks with python
functionality. An extension can be accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from
any operator within the extended component. If the extension is "promoted", all
its attributes with capitalized names can be accessed directly through the
extended component, e.g. op('yourComp').ExtensionMethod()
"""

class PickExt:
	"""
	ButtonSwitch description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.CallbackComp = None
		self.InfoPComp = None
		self.SetCallbackComponent()
		self.SetInfoPocessorModule()
		interactDat = self.ownerComp.op('config/interact')
		if interactDat:
			self.InteractDat = interactDat
		else:
			self.InteractDat = None
			raise Exception(self.ownerComp.path + '/config/interact : DAT not found')

	def HandleParPulse(self, par):
		"""
		Handles pulse parameter callbacks
		"""
		#print(par.name)
		if par.name == 'Editinfoprocessormodule':
			pMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
			if isinstance(pMod, DAT):
				pMod.par.edit.pulse()

	def SetCallbackComponent(self):
		"""
		Sets the callback component
		"""
		callbackComp = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackcomp.eval()
		if isinstance(callbackComp, COMP): 
			self.CallbackComp = callbackComp
		else:
			self.CallbackComp = None

	def SetInfoPocessorModule(self):
		"""
		Sets the info processor module
		"""
		infoPMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
		if isinstance(infoPMod, DAT): 
			self.InfoPComp = infoPMod
		else:
			self.InfoPComp = None

	@property
	def ParPadToggle(self):
		"""
		Returns values par Pad Toggle parameter
		"""
		return self.ownerComp.par.Padtoggle.eval()

	@property
	def ParSqueezeToggle(self):
		"""
		Returns values par Pad Toggle parameter
		"""
		return self.ownerComp.par.Squeezetoggle.eval()

	@property
	def ParTriggerToggle(self):
		"""
		Returns values par Pad Toggle parameter
		"""
		return self.ownerComp.par.Triggertoggle.eval()

	def Pad(self, info):
		"""
		Implements pad events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		buttonEvent = info['buttonEvent'] #['subEvent']
		buttonState = buttonEvent['buttonState']
		subEvent = buttonEvent.get('subEvent') 
		if subEvent:
			if buttonState:
				radioCell = self.InteractDat[picker+':pad:toggle:'+subEvent, 1]
				radioCell.val = 1 - int(radioCell.val)
			self.InteractDat[picker+':pad:momentary:'+subEvent, 1] = int(buttonState)
		else:
			self.InteractDat[picker+':pad:momentary',1] = int(buttonState)

		self.Processor(info)

	def Squeeze(self, info):
		"""
		Implements squeeze events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		buttonState = info['buttonEvent']['buttonState']
		if buttonState: 
			self.InteractDat[picker+':squeeze:toggle', 1] = 1 - int(self.InteractDat[picker+':squeeze:toggle', 1].val)
		self.InteractDat[picker+':squeeze:momentary', 1] = int(buttonState)
		self.Processor(info)

	def Trigger(self, info):
		"""
		Implements trigger events from picking system
		"""	
		picker = info['picker']
		buttonState = info['buttonEvent']['buttonState']
		if buttonState: 
			self.InteractDat[picker+':trigger:toggle', 1] = 1 - int(self.InteractDat[picker+':trigger:toggle', 1].val)
		self.InteractDat[picker+':trigger:momentary', 1] = int(buttonState)
		self.Processor(info)

	def Start(self, info):
		"""
		Implements enter events from picking system
		"""	
		picker = info['picker']
		self.InteractDat['hover:'+picker, 1] = 1
		self.Processor(info)

	def End(self, info):
		"""
		Implements exit events from picking system
		"""	
		picker = info['picker']
		self.InteractDat['hover:'+picker, 1] = 0
		self.Processor(info)

	def Hover(self, info):
		"""
		Implements hover events from picking system
		"""	
		self.Processor(info)
		
	def Processor(self, info):
		"""
		Checks for validity of callback function and if it exists passes along 
		interaction info dictionary to the specified processor component and function
		"""
		callFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackfunctionname.eval()
		procFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessorfunction.eval()
		#debug(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)

		if procFuncName and hasattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName):
			info = getattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)(info)
			if info:
				getattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName)(info)
				#if hasattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName):
				#	getattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName)(info)
				#else:
				#	raise Exception(self.CallbackComp.path, 'has no function', callFuncName, \
				#					'Check callback settings for', self.ownerComp.path)

*�����������������	*Ray Pick Component

The Ray Pick component takes a controllers component and references to cameras.  For each camera a ray is cast into 3D space and if a ray intersects with any of the geometry objects listed in the Pick Geos parameter, an array of chop channel data is provided describing the geometry at that ray cast position.  

In default VR setup a ray is cast by the "gaze" which is the direction of the center of the eyes from the HMD, as well and "control1" and "control2". 

In addition to generating pick information about picked objects, the rayPick extension scans pick objects for implemented python extensions and if a function that matches is found the funtion will be called, and an info object, which is a python dictionary filled with all the data from the pick object.

When a ray first detects a new object, the found object is searched for a Start() funtion. If the function is found the function is executed and passed the info dictionary.

When a ray casts on to the same object as the last cast, the inspected object is searched for a Hover() funtion. If the function is found the function is executed and passed  the info dictionary.

When a ray casts on to a new object, the previously cast object is searched for an End() funtion. If the function is found the function is executed and passed the info dictionary.

Controller Settings Parameters

Active: Activates the openVR sensor CHOP.

Contollers COMP: A controllers COMP is required because the controller channel data is comblined with the ray cast information when pick events are passed along to picked objects.

Pick Cameras: The cameras that are to cast rays into the 3D environment. The renderManager system finds all cameras tagged with "PickCam" and if this rayPick component is connected to a renderManager component, the Pick Cameras will be pushed onto this parameter.

Pick Geos: The objects that are to intersect with rays from the ray casters. The renderManager system finds all objects tagged with "PickGeo" and if this rayPick component is connected to a renderManager component, the Pick objects will be pushed onto this parameter.

Published OPs:

Pick CHOP: All the chop channels holding information on the current ray cast pick object and controller data. 

Pick DAT: All the DAT info holding information on the current ray cast pick object.

*�����������������z"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner component networks with python
functionality. An extension can be accessed via op('yourComp').ExtensionName.
If the extension is "promoted", all its attributes with capitalized names can
be accessed directly, e.g. op('yourComp').ExtensionMethod()
"""

from TdStoreTools import StorageManager
import os

class PickExt:
	"""
	PickExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		#The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp

		numPickCams = len(self.ownerComp.op('pickCams').col('camera')[1:])

		#This should be made more robust to handle whitespace issues in the string
		#pickCamNames = self.ownerComp.par.Pickcamnames.eval().split(' ')
		pickCamNames = [i.val for i in self.ownerComp.op('pickCamNames').col(0)]
		#debug('pickCamNames', pickCamNames)

		self.buttonEvents = self.ownerComp.par.Buttonevents.eval().split(' ')

		self.lastPickComps = {}
		self.lastPickEvents = {}

		for camName in pickCamNames:
			self.lastPickComps[camName] = None
			self.lastPickEvents[camName] = None

		# Set up storage for persistent data
		storedItems = []
		self.stored = StorageManager(self, ownerComp, storedItems)

	def HandlePick(self, info):
		"""
		handles pick events defined by pickCams defined by
		ownerComp.par.pickCamNames and manages the pickEvent for each pickCam.
		Determines if the event is a button event or simply a hover event and
		searches for implemented event functions on the picked component
		"""
		if hasattr(info['pickEvent'], 'pickOp'):
			pickOp = info['pickEvent'].pickOp
		else:
			pickOp = None
		picker = info['picker']
		if pickOp:
			pickComp = pickOp.parent()
			buttonName = info.get('buttonName')
			#If the buttonName is found in buttonEvents and a matching function
			#is found on pickComp then the function on the pick object is
			#executed
			if buttonName: #in self.buttonEvents:
				#debug(info.get('buttonName'), info)
				callback = getattr(pickComp, buttonName.capitalize(), None)
				if callback:
					callback(info)
			#If event info doesnt have a buttonName key the last pick object is
			#the same as current picck object then its a hover event
			elif pickComp == self.lastPickComps[picker]:
				#debug('hover', info)
				if hasattr(pickComp, 'Hover'):
					info['eventType'] = 'hover'
					pickComp.Hover(info)
			#If event info doesnt have a buttonName key the last pick object
			#doesnt match the current pick object then its a hover start end
			#event for the respective objects
			else:
				#try:
				#	lastPicked = self.lastPickComps[picker]
				#except:
				#	lastPickComps = self.lastPickComps[picker] = None
				try:
					endFunc = hasattr(self.lastPickComps[picker], 'End')
				except:
					endFunc = False
					self.lastPickComps[picker] = None
				if endFunc:
					info['eventType'] = 'end'
					self.lastPickComps[picker].End(info)
				if hasattr(pickComp, 'Start'):
					info['eventType'] = 'start'
					pickComp.Start(info)
			#When finished set the lastPickComp to pickComp
			self.lastPickComps[picker] = pickComp

		#This case deals with nothing getting picked
		elif self.lastPickComps[picker]:
			if hasattr(self.lastPickComps[picker], 'End'):
				info['eventType'] = 'end'
				self.lastPickComps[picker].End(info)
				#When finished set the lastPickComp to None
				self.lastPickComps[picker] = None

		self.lastPickEvents[picker] = info['pickEvent']

	def ButtonEvent(self, info):
		"""
		Creates a button event
		Adds button event info to the pick info dictionary
		"""
		controller = info['controller']
		button = info['button']
		picker = controller
		buttonState = info['buttonState']
		event = self.lastPickEvents[picker]
		newInfo = {'lastPickEvent': self.lastPickEvents[picker],
					'eventType':'button',
					'buttonName':button,
					'pickEvent':event,
					'buttonState':buttonState,
					'picker': picker,
					'buttonEvent':info
					 }
		#debug('eventcreate',info)
		self.HandlePick(newInfo)

#	v				- The selection v coordinate.			(float)
#	select			- True when a selection is ongoing.		(bool)
#	selectStart		- True at the start of a selection.		(bool)
#	selectEnd		- True at the end of a selection.		(bool)
#	selectedOp		- First picked operator.				(OP)
#	selectedTexture	- Texture coordinate of seleectedOp.	(Position)
#	pickOp			- Currently picked operator.			(OP)
#	pos				- 3D position of picked point.			(Position)
#	texture			- Texture coordinate of picked point.	(Position)

#	normal			- Geometry normal of picked point.		(Vector)
#	depth			- Post projection space depth.			(float)
#	instanceId		- Instance ID of the object.			(int)
#	row				- The row associated with this event	(float)
#	inValues		- Dictionary of input DAT strings for the given row, where keys are column headers. (dict)
import os

def onEvents(renderPickDat, events, eventsPrev):

	for event, eventPrev in zip(events, eventsPrev):
		#debug(event)
		picker = getPicker(event)		
		info = {'pickEvent':event}
		info['picker'] = picker
		parent.Device.HandlePick(info)

def getPicker(event):
	"""
	Extracts the picker which is currently extracted as the "name" defined by the custom parameter "Pick Cam Names"
	The number of names of this parameter must match the number of the pick camera parameters
	"""
	picker = event.inValues['name']
	#debug('picker', picker)
	return picker

*�����������������lfrom TdStoreTools import StorageManager

class ArcBallExt():

	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		'''
		Basic extension class for enhancing TouchDesigner component networks
		with python functionality
		'''
		#The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.arcInst = tdu.ArcBall(forCamera=True)
		self.matrix = tdu.Matrix()

		#The name for the dependency storage object is
		#this class name appended with 'Stored' ie. "ArcBallExtStored"
		storedItems = [
			{'name':'Empty','default':0},
			]

		#The stored attribute allows for easy access to the stored dependency dictionary
		self.stored = StorageManager(self, ownerComp, storedItems)

	def StartTransform(self,btn=None,u=0,v=0):
		if btn == 'lselect':
			#if lselect ==> rotate
			self.arcInst.beginRotate(u,v)
		elif btn == 'rselect':
			#if rselect ==> pan
			self.arcInst.beginPan(u,v)
		elif btn == 'mselect':
			#if mselect ==> zoom
			self.arcInst.beginDolly(u,v)

		return
		
		
	def Transform(self,btn=None,u=0,v=0,scaler=1):
		if btn == 'lselect':
			#if lselect ==> rotate
			self.arcInst.rotateTo(u,v,scale=scaler)
		elif btn == 'rselect':
			#if rselect ==> pan
			self.arcInst.panTo(u,v,scale=scaler)
		elif btn == 'mselect':
			#if mselect ==> zoom
			self.arcInst.dollyTo(u,v,scale=scaler)

		self.fillMat()
		return
		
	def Reset(self):
		op('autoRotate/hold3').par.pulse.pulse()
		self.arcInst.identity()
		self.fillMat()
		return
	
	def fillMat(self):
		newMat = self.arcInst.transform()
		self.matrix = newMat
		self.ownerComp.cook(force=True)
		for i in range(4):
			for j in range(4):
				op('newMat')[i,j] = newMat[i,j]

		return

?"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner component networks with python
functionality. An extension can be accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from
any operator within the extended component. If the extension is "promoted", all
its attributes with capitalized names can be accessed directly through the
extended component, e.g. op('yourComp').ExtensionMethod()
"""

import collections

class WorkstationExt:
	"""
	WorkstationExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.monitorsDat = self.ownerComp.op('monitors1') 
		self.valueNames = ['primary', 'left', 'right', 'bottom', 'top', 'width', 'height']
		#self.Monitor = collections.namedtuple('Monitor', self.valueNames)
		self.monitors = []

	def UpdateMonitors(self):
		"""
		Creates a named tuple for each monitor
		"""
		nMonitors = self.monitorsDat.numRows-1
	
		self.monitors = []
		for i in range(nMonitors):
			values = [int(self.monitorsDat[i+1,j].val) for j in self.valueNames]
			monitor = dict(zip(self.valueNames, values))
			self.monitors.append(monitor)

	def SnapToMonitor(self):
		"""
		Snaps the VR monitor to the specified screen index
		"""
		#monIndex = self.ownerComp.par.Capturemonitorindex.eval()
		primaryMon = next(monitor for monitor in self.monitors if monitor['primary'] == 1)
		#captureMon = self.monitors[monIndex]
		minX = min(monitor['left'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		minY = min(monitor['bottom'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		maxX = max(monitor['right'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		maxY = max(monitor['top'] for monitor in self.monitors)
		#debug(minX, minY, maxX, maxY)
		resolution = (maxX-minX, maxY-minY)
		#debug(resolution)

		#Set desktop crop
		left = abs(minX)
		right = left + primaryMon['width']
		bottom = abs(minY)
		top = bottom + primaryMon['height']
		self.SetDesktopCrop(left, right, bottom, top)

		#Set screen crop match default monitor
		left = 0
		right = primaryMon['width']
		bottom = 0
		top = primaryMon['height']
		self.SetScreenCrop(left, right, bottom, top)

	def SetDesktopCrop(self, left, right, bottom, top):
		"""
		Sets the crop settings
		"""
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcropleft = left
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcropright = right
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcropbottom = bottom
		self.ownerComp.par.Desktopcroptop = top

	def SetScreenCrop(self, left, right, bottom, top):
		"""
		Sets screen crop 
		"""

		self.ownerComp.par.Screencropleft = left
		self.ownerComp.par.Screencropright = right
		self.ownerComp.par.Screencropbottom = bottom
		self.ownerComp.par.Screencroptop = top

class PickExt:
	"""
	ButtonSwitch description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.CallbackComp = None
		self.InfoPComp = None
		self.SetCallbackComponent()
		self.SetInfoPocessorModule()

	def HandleParPulse(self, par):
		"""
		Handles pulse parameter callbacks
		"""
		print(par.name)
		if par.name == 'Editinfoprocessormodule':
			pMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
			if isinstance(pMod, DAT):
				pMod.par.edit.pulse()

	def SetCallbackComponent(self):
		"""
		Sets the callback component
		"""
		callbackComp = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackcomp.eval()
		if isinstance(callbackComp, COMP): 
			self.CallbackComp = callbackComp
		else:
			self.CallbackComp = None

	def SetInfoPocessorModule(self):
		"""
		Sets the info processor module
		"""
		infoPMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
		if isinstance(infoPMod, DAT): 
			self.InfoPComp = infoPMod
		else:
			self.InfoPComp = None

	def Trigger(self, info):
		"""
		Responds to trigger events from picking system
		"""	
		debug(info)
		callFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackfunctionname.eval()
		procFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessorfunction.eval()
		#debug(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)
		if procFuncName and hasattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName):
			info = getattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)(info)
			if info:
				getattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName)(info)

*�����������������/"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner component networks with python
functionality. An extension can be accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from
any operator within the extended component. If the extension is "promoted", all
its attributes with capitalized names can be accessed directly through the
extended component, e.g. op('yourComp').ExtensionMethod()
"""

class PickExt:
	"""
	ButtonSwitch description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.CallbackComp = None
		self.InfoPComp = None
		self.SetCallbackComponent()
		self.SetInfoPocessorModule()
		interactDat = self.ownerComp.op('config/interact')
		if interactDat:
			self.InteractDat = interactDat
		else:
			self.InteractDat = None
			raise Exception(self.ownerComp.path + '/config/interact : DAT not found')

	def HandleParPulse(self, par):
		"""
		Handles pulse parameter callbacks
		"""
		#print(par.name)
		if par.name == 'Editinfoprocessormodule':
			pMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
			if isinstance(pMod, DAT):
				pMod.par.edit.pulse()

	def SetCallbackComponent(self):
		"""
		Sets the callback component
		"""
		callbackComp = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackcomp.eval()
		if isinstance(callbackComp, COMP):
			self.CallbackComp = callbackComp
		else:
			self.CallbackComp = None

	def SetInfoPocessorModule(self):
		"""
		Sets the info processor module
		"""
		infoPMod = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessormodule.eval()
		if isinstance(infoPMod, DAT):
			self.InfoPComp = infoPMod
		else:
			self.InfoPComp = None

	@property
	def ParPadToggle(self):
		"""
		Returns values par Pad Toggle parameter
		"""
		return self.ownerComp.par.Padtoggle.eval()

	@property
	def ParSqueezeToggle(self):
		"""
		Returns values par Pad Toggle parameter
		"""
		return self.ownerComp.par.Squeezetoggle.eval()

	@property
	def ParTriggerToggle(self):
		"""
		Returns values par Pad Toggle parameter
		"""
		return self.ownerComp.par.Triggertoggle.eval()

	def Pad(self, info):
		"""
		Implements pad events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		buttonEvent = info['buttonEvent'] #['subEvent']
		buttonState = buttonEvent['buttonState']
		subEvent = buttonEvent.get('subEvent')
		if subEvent:
			if buttonState:
				radioCell = self.InteractDat[picker+':pad:toggle:'+subEvent, 1]
				radioCell.val = 1 - int(radioCell.val)
			self.InteractDat[picker+':pad:momentary:'+subEvent, 1] = int(buttonState)
		else:
			self.InteractDat[picker+':pad:momentary',1] = int(buttonState)

		self.Processor(info)

	def Squeeze(self, info):
		"""
		Implements squeeze events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		buttonState = info['buttonEvent']['buttonState']
		if buttonState:
			self.InteractDat[picker+':squeeze:toggle', 1] = 1 - int(self.InteractDat[picker+':squeeze:toggle', 1].val)
		self.InteractDat[picker+':squeeze:momentary', 1] = int(buttonState)
		self.Processor(info)

	def Trigger(self, info):
		"""
		Implements trigger events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		buttonState = info['buttonEvent']['buttonState']
		if buttonState:
			self.InteractDat[picker+':trigger:toggle', 1] = 1 - int(self.InteractDat[picker+':trigger:toggle', 1].val)
		self.InteractDat[picker+':trigger:momentary', 1] = int(buttonState)
		self.Processor(info)

	def Start(self, info):
		"""
		Implements enter events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		self.InteractDat['hover:'+picker, 1] = 1
		self.Processor(info)

	def End(self, info):
		"""
		Implements exit events from picking system
		"""
		picker = info['picker']
		self.InteractDat['hover:'+picker, 1] = 0
		self.Processor(info)

	def Hover(self, info):
		"""
		Implements hover events from picking system
		"""
		self.Processor(info)

	def Processor(self, info):
		"""
		Checks for validity of callback function and if it exists passes along
		interaction info dictionary to the specified processor component and function
		"""
		callFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Callbackfunctionname.eval()
		procFuncName = self.ownerComp.par.Infoprocessorfunction.eval()
		#debug(procFuncName)

		if procFuncName and hasattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName):
			info = getattr(mod(self.InfoPComp), procFuncName)(info)
			if self.CallbackComp:
				callFunc = getattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName)
				if info and callFunc:
					callFunc(info)
				#if hasattr(self.CallbackComp, callFuncName):

*�����������������# me - this DAT
# scriptOp - the OP which is cooking

# press 'Setup Parameters' in the OP to call this function to re-create the parameters.
#def setupParameters(scriptOp):
#	return

# called whenever custom pulse parameter is pushed
def onPulse(par):
	return

def cook(scriptOp):
	scriptOp.clear()
	pxChan = scriptOp.inputs[0]['p1_X']
	pyChan = scriptOp.inputs[0]['p1_Y']

	padUp = scriptOp.appendChan('up')
	padDown = scriptOp.appendChan('down')
	padLeft = scriptOp.appendChan('left')
	padRight = scriptOp.appendChan('right')

	padUp[0] = 0
	padDown[0] = 0
	padLeft[0] = 0
	padRight[0] = 0

	if pxChan[0] > 0.99:
		padRight[0] = 1
	elif pyChan[0] > 0.99:
		padUp[0] = 1
	elif pyChan[0] < 0.01 and not (pxChan[0] < 0.01):
		padDown[0] = 1
	elif pxChan[0] < 0.01:
		padLeft[0] = 1

	return
